document.title: Usability Validation Plan for ADD(HAD1717MCW / HAD1417MCW) Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation test plan for ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector models HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines usability test participants, scenarios, acceptance criteria, goals, and reporting approach for ADD usability validation.
keyPoints: The document covers ADD accessories HAD1417MCW and HAD1717MCW and states performance and usage are the same except for size.
Seven participants are specified for the usability test with defined education, knowledge, language, and experience profile criteria.
Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
The usability goal is 100% completion rate for each usability task and below 4 total errors for each task.
Referenced standards include EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Critical error
concepts.evidence: A critical error is defined as an error that results in an incorrect or incomplete outcome.

concepts.name: Non-critical error
concepts.evidence: A non-critical error is an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Summative evaluation hazard-related use scenarios
concepts.evidence: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation

concepts.name: Usability engineering for medical devices
concepts.evidence: EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices

evidence.claim: The document is titled as a usability validation plan for ADD HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for ADD(HAD1717MCW / HAD1417MCW) Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-039 and Rev.0.
The document date associated with preparation, approval, and revision establishment is Nov 7 2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D Manager Lee, Dongho Nov 7 2023; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Nov 7 2023; Rev. date 0 Nov 7 2023 Establishment

evidence.claim: The document describes a test plan for conducting usability testing during ADD development.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the ADD.

evidence.claim: HAD1417MCW and HAD1717MCW have the same performance and usage except for size, with no usability difference stated.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'

evidence.claim: The usability test specifies a total of 7 participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants must meet profile criteria covering education, legal qualification knowledge, English understanding, and no required special experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: At least two years of university, equal or more; Knowledge: User who meet the legal qualification for each country; Language understanding: User who are understand the English; Experience: Not require any special experience

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: Subjective evaluations use questionnaires and a 5-point satisfaction rating scale from 1 strongly disagree to 5 strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: The questionnaires will utilize free-form responses and rating scales. Post-test satisfaction questionnaire Rating: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating usability scenarios include user manual review, cable and power connections, powering on the detector, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions. Familiarize yourself with the user manual before and after the product; Connecting the power cord to the power supply; Connecting the Data Cable to the Power Supply and the Detector; Power on the detector; Arrange the patient in the correct position to have detector aligned with the target body part; Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Hazard-related use scenarios include user manual checking, preparation, product use, X-ray image implementation, maintenance, cleaning, and battery charging/change.
evidence.evidenceText: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation Scenario Task Potential for use errors ... Check the User manual ... Preparation for using the product ... Product use ... Implementation of X-ray image ... Maintenance Cleaning ... Battery charging and change

evidence.claim: The usability goal is 100% completion rate for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for errors is below 4 total errors for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The reporting output is a usability verification and evaluation report or presentation evaluating metrics against pre-approved goals and recommendations.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test. It will consist of a report and/or a presentation of the results; evaluate the usability metrics against the pre-approved goals, subjective evaluations, and specific usability problems and recommendations for resolution.

evidence.claim: The document references EN 60601-1-6:2010 for collateral usability requirements for medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The document references EN 62366-1:2015 for application of usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices

evidence.claim: The document references IEC TR 62366-2:2016 for guidance on application of usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC TR 62366-2:2016 Medical devices — Part 2: Guidance on the application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document references EN ISO 14971:2019 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; authorityOrJurisdiction is therefore empty.
Acceptance criteria repeatedly state 'over 100% of operators', which appears internally ambiguous or mathematically inconsistent but is preserved as source wording.
Some task wording appears inconsistent or typographical, such as 'date cable' and 'Cable code connection'; extracted claims use only clearly supported content.
